hero.badge: Tiny gnomes. Big homes. Stealth heists with friends.
hero.title: Burglin' Gnomes wiki
hero.description: Your hub for Burglin' Gnomes: how the heists work, High-Gnome jobs, crafting & upgrades, enemies, controls, and the fastest ways to survive each run.

gameFeature.title: Burglin' Gnomes Gameplay
gameFeature.description: Watch tiny gnomes pull off big heists in chaotic 6-player co-op stealth missions!

demoDownload.title: Burglin' Gnomes Demo Download
demoDownload.subtitle: Get the free demo on Steam

sections.heading: Where to download
items: Open the official Steam store page and use the 'Download Demo' section.
If Steam asks you to sign in, sign in first and try again.

sections.heading: Install & launch quick checks
items: Default install location: Steam Library folder → steamapps → common → (game folder).
If it won't launch: verify the game files in Steam, then restart Steam.

beginnerGuide.title: Burglin' Gnomes Beginner Guide
beginnerGuide.subtitle: Master the core loop and avoid common mistakes

steps.title: Enter the House
steps.description: Drop into a human house from your floating island base. Spawn outside and sneak in through unlocked doors or windows.

steps.title: Complete Tasks
steps.description: Finish High-Gnome tasks before time runs out. Check objectives via Tab key and prioritize risky tasks first.

steps.title: Steal Items
steps.description: Grab valuables and materials for crafting. Avoid making noise or getting spotted by the homeowner.

steps.title: Escape via Tornado
steps.description: When the timer ends, a tornado spawns in the yard—reach it to extract safely. Start moving early!

steps.title: Upgrade & Repeat
steps.description: Use rewards and materials to craft gear back home, then start your next heist with better equipment.

pitfalls.title: Getting Caught
pitfalls.description: Homeowner will chase you. Stay hidden, use distractions, and break line of sight to escape.

pitfalls.title: Running Out of Time
pitfalls.description: Tornado timer is strict. Start heading to extraction early instead of over-looting.

pitfalls.title: Ignoring Teammates
pitfalls.description: Rescue trapped allies from fridge/oven traps quickly—call out immediately for saves.

coopGuide.title: Burglin' Gnomes Co-op Guide
coopGuide.subtitle: Coordinate with up to 6 players for maximum chaos

roles.name: Scout
roles.description: Check for threats, mark safe paths, and guide team movement

roles.name: Carrier
roles.description: Focus on heavy items with backpack upgrades and secure task progress

roles.name: Distraction
roles.description: Lure homeowner/pets away from team to create safe windows

roles.name: Rescuer
roles.description: Free trapped teammates from fridge/oven before they die

coopGuide.communication: Use voice chat (V key) to coordinate tasks and rescues. Call out: 'Spotted', 'Route', 'Need rescue', 'Extract soon'.

tasksObjectives.title: Burglin' Gnomes Tasks & Objectives
tasksObjectives.subtitle: What the High-Gnome wants: task examples

examples: Grab specific items marked by High-Gnome objectives
Navigate to specified areas inside the house

efficiency: Do risky tasks first while the team is together
Assign one carrier for task items so they don't get lost during chases
Start moving toward extraction before time expires

controlsKeybinds.title: Burglin' Gnomes Controls & Keybinds
controlsKeybinds.subtitle: Default keyboard controls (and where to change them)
controlsKeybinds.changePath: From title menu: open SETTINGS, then check keyboard settings. Use search field to filter keybind rows.

contentSettings.title: Burglin' Gnomes Content Settings
contentSettings.subtitle: Mature content, nudity toggle, and Steam preferences

details.heading: What the developers state
items: A human may appear nude while doing activities like bathing.
Nudity is censored and can be fully turned off optionally.
Gnomes can be dismembered and die in brutal ways.

details.heading: If Steam blocks the community page
items: Steam may hide the community hub depending on your content preferences.
Use Steam's 'Update Content Preferences' to adjust what you allow Steam to show.

craftingEquipment.title: Burglin' Gnomes Crafting & Equipment
craftingEquipment.subtitle: Gear up: crafting examples and what each tool does

equipment.benefit: Expands inventory capacity for more loot per run
Lets you open doors and interact with more objects
Prevents fall damage from high drops during escapes
Helps you fight threats like mice when cornered

sections.heading: How crafting connects to looting
items: During a run, steal items and bring them back as crafting materials.
After extraction, rewards/materials support new crafts.

sections.heading: Base improvement
items: Craft furniture and upgrade your floating-island home (full game feature).

lootItems.title: Burglin' Gnomes Loot & Items
lootItems.subtitle: What to steal: item types, risk, and carry tips

examples: Items required by High-Gnome for objectives
Household items repurposed into gnome gear and upgrades
Objects involved in tasks like breaking or flooding

carryTips: Prioritize task progress, then fill remaining capacity with crafting materials
If threats are nearby, move items in short safe hops rather than long exposed carries
Plan the route to the yard extraction before the timer ends

riskLevels.description: Deeper rooms or near roaming threats
Close to homeowner patrol or robot vacuum

threats.name: The Homeowner
threats.trigger: Getting spotted while burgling or doing tasks
threats.behavior: He chases and tries to catch you
threats.counter: Stay stealthy, break sightlines, and keep moving as a team

threats.name: Cats
threats.trigger: Getting too close or crossing their path
threats.behavior: They become a direct threat while you're sneaking
threats.counter: Avoid their routes and move quietly past

threats.name: Mice
threats.behavior: They threaten fragile gnomes during runs
threats.counter: Stay alert in tight spaces and be ready to retreat

threats.name: Robot Vacuum
threats.counter: Listen for it, keep off its route, and reposition fast

threats.name: Fridge / Oven Trap
threats.behavior: You can be locked inside; if teammates don't rescue in time, you die
threats.counter: Call out immediately; teammates prioritize the rescue route

threats.name: Time Limit
threats.trigger: The run timer reaching the limit
threats.behavior: Extraction tornado appears in yard; you must reach it to escape
threats.counter: Stop over-looting, regroup, and commit to extraction early

stealthEscape.title: Burglin' Gnomes Stealth & Escape Tips
stealthEscape.subtitle: Steal more, die less
do: Stay stealthy and take cover before interacting with objects
Use teamwork: call out threats and coordinate routes
Rescue teammates quickly when someone is trapped or down
Cut your losses and head for extraction when the timer is tight
dont: Get greedy when extraction is coming up
Split too far from the team when the homeowner is active
Ignore robot vacuum paths or animal patrols
Delay rescues when a teammate is locked in

quickTips.title: The 3-Second Rule
quickTips.text: If you can't explain your next move in 3 seconds, you're about to get spotted.

quickTips.title: Rescue First
quickTips.text: A fast rescue keeps the run alive; looting can wait.

quickTips.title: Extraction Discipline
quickTips.text: Regroup early and commit to the tornado before panic starts.

branches.summary: Carry more and bring more back
Interact with the house more effectively
items: Gnome Gloves (lets you open doors and more)

branches.summary: Deal with small threats when needed
Craft furniture and upgrade your floating-island home

priority.title: Storage first
priority.reason: More carry capacity improves every run's payoff

priority.title: Utility next
priority.reason: Better interactions reduce time spent in danger zones

priority.title: Mobility for survival
priority.reason: Fall safety prevents avoidable deaths when escaping

modsBepInEx.title: Burglin' Gnomes Mods & BepInEx Setup
modsBepInEx.subtitle: Install mods the clean way

steps.title: Install BepInEx 5
steps.text: Download BepInEx 5 and extract it into the game root folder.

steps.title: Launch once to generate folders
steps.text: Start the game to the main menu, then close it. This creates the BepInEx folder structure.

steps.title: Install mods
steps.text: Place mod .dll files into the plugins folder.

steps.title: Verify the install
steps.text: Re-launch the game and confirm the mod feature is available (camera toggle, UI cursor, etc.).

modsBepInEx.multiplayerNote: For modded co-op gameplay features, everyone should install BepInEx and the same gameplay mods.

mods.title: Spawn Menu
mods.description: Adds an item spawn menu with a flying mode.
mods.note: Host-only; others do not need to install it to join.

mods.title: More Hats
mods.description: Adds additional hat color options for gnomes.

mods.title: DOOM on TV
mods.description: Play original DOOM on an in-game TV, with sound coming through the TV speaker.
highlights: Works in multiplayer so other players can spectate on the same TV
Includes the shareware DOOM episode data (Episode 1)

controllerSupport.title: Burglin' Gnomes Controller Support
controllerSupport.subtitle: Full gamepad play

native.title: In-Game Settings
native.description: Use the game's bindings/settings menus to adjust controls.

mod.title: Controller Support Mod
mod.description: Adds complete gamepad/controller support and a UI cursor for menu navigation.

features: Full Gameplay Controls: movement, camera, combat, interactions, and abilities mapped to gamepad
UI Cursor Navigation: a white circle cursor appears in menus; press A to click and drag sliders
Inventory Navigation: hold Select to open inventory; use right stick to navigate while holding it
Scrollable Menus: right stick scrolls crafting recipes, settings, and other scrollable UI areas
Hotbar Cycling: D-pad left/right cycles through 4 hotbar slots
Seamless Input Switching: auto-detects keyboard/mouse vs controller

milestones.title: Demo released on Steam
milestones.detail: Public demo became available on PC (Steam).

milestones.title: All-time peak reached
milestones.detail: The demo hit its highest recorded concurrent players.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Burglin' Gnomes

questions.question: How do I download the Burglin' Gnomes demo?
questions.answer: Visit the Steam store page and click 'Download Demo'. The demo is free and requires 2.62 GiB to download, 4.88 GiB disk space.

questions.question: Can I play Burglin' Gnomes solo or do I need friends?
questions.answer: Burglin' Gnomes supports both solo play and co-op with up to 6 players. Solo is harder but possible.

questions.question: What are High-Gnome tasks in Burglin' Gnomes?
questions.answer: High-Gnome is your boss who assigns objectives like 'break the TV', 'flood the toilet', or 'steal specific items'. Complete them for extra rewards.

questions.question: How do I install mods for Burglin' Gnomes?
questions.answer: Install BepInEx 5, launch the game once, then place mod DLLs in the BepInEx/plugins/ folder. All players need the same mods for multiplayer.

questions.question: Does Burglin' Gnomes support controllers?
questions.answer: Yes! Use in-game settings for basic controller support, or install the Controller Mod from Nexus Mods for full UI navigation.

questions.question: What is the player count for Burglin' Gnomes?
questions.answer: As of February 2026, Burglin' Gnomes averages 6,000+ concurrent players, with a peak of 15,272 on February 8, 2026.

cta.description: Join the Burglin' Gnomes community on Discord for co-op groups, updates, and mod discussions!
